title: (SACRAMENTO) – Assemblymember Steve Bennett (D-Ventura) and representatives from Community Water Center (CWC) announced legislation today to strengthen the Sustainable Groundwater

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "New water wells and groundwater extractions are being approved without adequate analysis of their impact on the drinking water of disadvantaged communities.",
    "Approval without adequate analysis can cause significant negative impacts on over-drafted water basins and disadvantaged communities' drinking water.",
    "Access to safe and affordable drinking water is a human right that remains unrealized for too many Californians.",
    "Residents of low-income communities of color that rely on small water systems and domestic wells are particularly affected by inadequate drinking water access.",
    "The well permitting process must be strengthened to ensure that new agricultural well drilling is consistent with sustainable groundwater management.",
    "Increased state and local oversight is necessary to prevent over-pumping of groundwater, which can cause shallow domestic and community water wells to run dry.",
    "The over-pumping of groundwater threatens the water supply of disadvantaged communities throughout the state.",
    "The goals of the Sustainable Groundwater Management Act (SGMA) include ensuring the Human Right to Water.",
    "The challenges of Climate Change will affect the future water supply in California."
  ]
}
```
